title: trifolium pratense subsp. serotinum
intro: Trifolium pratense subsp. serotinum, also known as the late red clover, is a subspecies of the red clover plant (Trifolium pratense), a legume native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and estrogenic effects, making it useful in treating menopausal symptoms and other hormone-related issues. The health benefits of Trifolium pratense subsp. serotinum include reducing hot flashes, improving sleep quality, and alleviating symptoms of osteoporosis, as well as supporting cardiovascular health and immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in the form of capsules, teas, and tinctures, although its use should be supervised by a healthcare professional due to potential interactions with other medications.
